data.title: Perplexity reports extreme growth as CEO says users 'are tired of legacy browsers': report
data.description:
Perplexity's artificial intelligence-powered search engine is recording 20% month-over-month growth, with queries reaching 780M in May, said its CEO Aravind Srinivas on Thursday, according to a report by TechCrunch.

Srinivas, speaking during the Bloomberg Tech Summit, said his search engine will reach 1B queries per week if it can sustain its current growth rate, the report said. He also said internet surfers are tired of using the same old browsers, such as Google's (NASDAQ:GOOGL)(NASDAQ:GOOG) Chrome.

"If people are in the browser, it's infinite retention," Srinivas said, according to the report. "Everything in the search bar, everything on the new tab page, everything you're doing on the sidecar, any of the pages you're in, these are all going to be extra queries per active user, as well as seeking new users who just are tired of legacy browsers, like Chrome."  

Perplexity is gearing up to release Comet, which is a type of agentic search engine that can complete user tasks. Those interested can join a waitlist here.

"Comet will have a native virtual meets recording, transcription and searches over them," Srinivas posted last month on X. "Won't be part of the first release, but very fast follow up. As for release date: it's going to take a min of three weeks and a max of five weeks. Reliability and latency have improved over the last few weeks. The last 10-20% is so much harder than we thought. Everyone's working super hard to get the browser out in your hands. Bear with us until then."

Perplexity, which is backed by major investors such as Nvidia (NVDA) and Amazon (AMZN) co-founder Jeff Bezos, is reportedly in the late stages of a new funding round that would value the startup at $14B. It was valued at $9B in December.

The search engine added a new feature last month that allows users to buy products directly in the chat, utilizing PayPal (PYPL) or Venmo, for the checkout process.

Despite the rising popularity of new search engines such as Perplexity, Google's search engine continues to hold about 90% of the global market share, according to various reports. Microsoft's (MSFT) Bing ranks second with about 4%.

data.title: BlackRock said to plan its second round of layoffs this year
data.description:
BlackRock (NYSE:BLK) is planning to lay off about 300 staffers in its second round of job cuts this year, Bloomberg reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

The layoffs this month will represent slightly more than 1% of its total headcount, which stood at ~22.6K at the end of March.

The asset management giant has expanded its workforce by 14% since end-2023, Bloomberg noted, as it absorbs employees coming from a recent large acquisition.

data.title: Toll Brothers prices $500M in 5.600% senior notes due 2035
data.description:
• Toll Brothers (NYSE:TOL) has priced a $500M public offering of 5.600% senior notes due 2035.
  • The notes are issued by Toll Brothers Finance Corp. and are expected to settle on June 10, 2025.
  • Interest will be paid semi-annually on June 15 and December 15, beginning December 15, 2025.
  • Proceeds will be used for general corporate purposes, including potential repayment of 4.875% senior notes due 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: SpaceX plans to decommission the spacecraft used to take astronauts to the ISS following a Trump threat
data.description:
Elon Musk warned on Thursday that SpaceX will begin decommissioning its Dragon spacecraft “immediately” because of an overt threat by President Donald Trump to cancel government contracts with Musk’s businesses.

"In light of the President’s statement about cancellation of my government contracts, @SpaceX will begin decommissioning its Dragon spacecraft immediately," Musk wrote.

That X post was in response to Trump saying on Truth Social that the easiest way for the U.S. government to save billions was to terminate the government subsidies and contracts with Musk's companies. 

The SpaceX (SPACE) Dragoncraft is a family of partially reusable spacecraft developed, manufactured, and operated by the company. The Crew Dragon variant was designed to transport astronauts to and from the International Space Station and other destinations in low-Earth orbit, while the Cargo Dragon variant is used to deliver supplies, equipment, and scientific experiments to the ISS.

The Russian Soyuz and Northrop Grumman (NOC) Cygnus have also supplied the ISS. A partnership between the European Space Agency and Japan's JAXA has also provided cargo resupply missions in the past.

The ISS resupply spacecraft was pulled into what was a daylong battle between Elon Musk and Donald Trump that began as a disagreement over the spending bill pending in the U.S. Senate.

data.title: U.S. Treasury monitors nine trading partners for currency practices, macro policies
data.description:
The U.S. Treasury said on Thursday it created a monitoring list of nine major trading partners to keep an their currency practices and macroeconomic policies.

The monitoring list includes China, Japan, Korea, Taiwan, Singapore, Vietnam, Germany, Ireland, and Switzerland.

In its semi-annual report to Congress, the Treasury found that no major trading partner manipulated its exchange rate vs. the U.S. dollar for gaining an unfair advantage in international trade during the four quarters through December 2024.

It also found that no major trading partner met all three criteria for enhanced analysis under the Trade Facilitation and Trade Enforcement Act of 2015 during the four quarters ending December 2024.  

"While Treasury has not designated China as a currency manipulator in this report amid RMB depreciation pressure, China stands out among our major trading partners in its lack of transparency around its exchange rate policies and practices," the Treasury said in a statement.

Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent commented, "In line with President Trump’s America First Trade Policy, the United States Treasury will be vigilant in identifying and taking action against currency manipulation and will continue to closely monitor a range of relevant macroeconomic and financial policies implemented by our trading partners that propagate imbalances, contribute to significant exchange rate misalignments, or result in an unfair competitive advantage in trade.”

In the report, the Department reviewed and assessed the policies of major U.S. trading partners, comprising ~78% of U.S. foreign trade in goods and services during the four quarters through December 2024.

The Treasury will continue to monitor whether the U.S.'s trading partners' policies are acting through foreign exchange intervention or non-market policies and practices to manipulate their currencies to gain an unfair advantage in trade.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Visa snaps seven straight sessions of gains
data.description:
Visa (NYSE:V) snapped seven straight sessions of gains as the stock closed 0.33% lower, at $366.77 on Thursday.

The San Francisco-based payment technology company gained around 2.5% in the past seven trading sessions. Visa is up 5.07% over the past one month. The stock closed 0.58% higher, at $368 on Wednesday.

Mizuho Americas upgraded Visa (NYSE:V) to Outperform from Neutral on the premise that the U.S. consumers will increase their spending in categories where they're more likely to pay with a card than cash.

The brokerage firm estimates that U.S. card penetration is at ~75%, compared with the consensus of 80%-90%.

"This leaves room for another decade of solid top-line growth domestically," Mizuho analyst Dan Dolev wrote in a note to clients, increasing V’s price target to $425 from $359.

Seeking Alpha authors and the sell-side analysts see the stock as a BUY, while the Quant rating system has graded the stock as a HOLD.

The stock has gained around 16.70% so far this year, outperforming the broader S&P500 index, which rose about 1.52% during the same period.
